threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to Broadway Market instead of just knowing which alley leads there.
Caught a girl on the 59 last night trying to braid her mate's hair with one hand while holding a takeaway in the other. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The sound of a neighbor's saxophone cutting through the morning chaos of a bus strike—classic Neukölln vibes that remind you why you're here.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you hear the U-Bahn drivers announcing stops in a mix of German and Turkish—it's like a mini cultural exchange underground.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they correct you on the pronunciation of ‘Valencia’ while ordering a burrito. Tourists just butcher it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 19 Polk singing opera at the top of his lungs. Tourists were confused, but locals just rolled their eyes and nodded. Classic Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to park Güell but miss the tiny, vibrant plazas in Gràcia where the locals actually kick back with a caña, completely ignoring the real charm.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still stop to take photos of every street corner—like they’re discovering a hidden gem, even in a rainy day.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when the bus driver shouts 'Next stop!' instead of saying where you are. Give it a week, you’ll be in on the secret.
Caught a glimpse of a couple arguing over a half-eaten kebab on the 133 last night. He swore it was 'extra spicy'—she just rolled her eyes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a toddler feeding pigeons while his dad argued with a street artist over the price of a mural. Just a normal day in our corner.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a playground with an outdoor beer garden right next to the swings. Parents sip while kids swing—Berlin multitasking at its best!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they react when a BART delay ruins their plans—just a resigned eye roll, not a surprise. It’s part of life here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy fitting a whole cat tree into the back of a crowded 19 bus. No one batted an eye. Just another day in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all hipster bars, but many locals still do their shopping in the family-owned markets, not the fancy boutiques.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they line up at the bakery for fresh baguettes at 8 AM, while locals already know to grab them the night before.
